headline: HubSpot and Other Software Stocks Are Bouncing Back. Their Next Move Could Come Soon.
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector Exchange-Traded fund—which owns large software names such as  Microsoft Oracle Salesforce  Adobe, and  Palantir  —is up 12% since late February, after a brutal monthslong selloff.  The market is concerned that the privately-owned AI pioneers Anthropic and OpenAI will take market share from the many software vendors that provide tools to businesses and people.  The industry’s early recovery comes as bargain hunters take a chance on these beaten-down names, betting that some can still thrive despite AI.

headline: Why proving AI growth drivers is crucial for software landscape
summary: CrowdStrike stock (CRWD) is on a rollercoaster ride Wednesday morning after the cybersecurity firm reported fourth quarter results that just beat Wall Street's earnings and revenue estimates. Founder and CEO George Kurtz said that the company's fiscal 2026 will "go down in our history books as CrowdStrike's best year yet." RBC Capital Markets managing director and head of global TIMT research Matt Hedberg comes on the program to discuss CrowdStrike's guidance and how important AI will be for driving growth in the software landscape. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: CrowdStrike ARR Tops US$5b As Identity And AI Security Expand
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (NasdaqGS:CRWD) reported a record fiscal year, reaching more than $5b in annual recurring revenue. The company launched FalconID, a next generation, passwordless authentication product with AI resistant multifactor authentication. CrowdStrike announced a partnership with VAST Data and NVIDIA focused on securing data across the full AI workflow. CrowdStrike enters this phase with its shares at $391.42 and a long run track record that includes a 3 year return of 213.3%...
